Class Table

java.lang.Object
Entities.Table
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Table extends Object implements Serializable
Represents a table in the restaurant. A table has an ID, a seating capacity, and availability status. It provides methods to check if it can seat a given number of guests, and to occupy or release the table.
See Also:

    Description
    private boolean
    available
    True if the table is available, false if occupied.

    capacity
    Number of seats available at the table.
    private int
    tableID
    Unique identifier for the table.

  • Field Details

    • tableID

      private int tableID
      Unique identifier for the table.

    • capacity

      private int capacity
      Number of seats available at the table.

    • available

      private boolean available
      True if the table is available, false if occupied.

  • Constructor Details

    • Table

      public Table(int tableID, int capacity)
      Constructs a new table with a given ID and capacity. The table is initially available.
      Parameters:
      tableID - the table's unique identifier
      capacity - the number of seats at the table

    • Table

      public Table(int tableID, int capacity, boolean available)
      Constructs a table with a given ID, capacity, and availability. This constructor is typically used when loading from the database.
      Parameters:
      tableID - the table's unique identifier
      capacity - the number of seats at the table
      available - true if the table is available, false if occupied

  • Method Details

    • getTableID

      public int getTableID()
      Returns the table ID.

    • getCapacity

      public int getCapacity()
      Returns the table's seating capacity.

    • isAvailable

      public boolean isAvailable()
      Returns true if the table is available.

    • setCapacity

      public void setCapacity(int newCapacity)
      Sets the table's seating capacity.

    • canSeat

      public boolean canSeat(int numOfGuests)
      Checks if the table can seat a given number of guests.
      Parameters:
      numOfGuests - the number of guests
      Returns:
      true if the table is available and has enough seats

    • occupy

      public void occupy()
      Marks the table as occupied.

    • release

      public void release()
      Marks the table as available.
